description: Itemized, expiring baseline of KNOWN, milestone-owned security findings. NOT a waiver and NOT a rule-family suppression: every entry is a single finding with a stable fingerprint, an owning production gate + milestone, a removal criterion, and an expiry. Each entry declares a truthful evidenceType; a `semgrep` entry must reconcile to an actual Semgrep result, `dependency_audit` entries are reconciled by scripts/check-dependency-audit.mjs against npm audit --json, `gitleaks` entries carry an EXACT expectedFindings inventory reconciled finding-by-finding (rule+path+line) against a live gitleaks report (no directory waiver) with every containing file additionally bound to its whole-file Git blob OID so an in-place same-line substitution fails, and `manual_static` entries assert a still-present repository path. A changed rule/path/fingerprint/chain, or any NEW Critical/High, fails CI. No wildcards.

reachabilityEnum.browser_runtime: present/loaded in the shipped static client bundle
reachabilityEnum.server_runtime: loaded by application server code (server/**) at Node runtime; note that no server is deployed in production (static SPA) — the DEV sidecars are the only servers
reachabilityEnum.build_only: loaded only by build/bundler tooling; not in shipped output
reachabilityEnum.dev_only: loaded only by dev tooling not part of the app server (test/lint/dev CLI)
reachabilityEnum.not_reachable: present in the lockfile but not loaded by any browser, server, or build code path
reachabilityEnum.unresolved: reachability could not be proven from static analysis

policy.reachabilityRule: Absence from the browser bundle alone NEVER downgrades a finding to not_reachable; a Node/server dependency loaded by a running server is server_runtime even if the specific exploit precondition is unmet. Severity and blocker status are never weakened on reachability grounds.

findings.rationale: DEV-only Shipping sidecar label-proxy fetches a request-controlled URL. The Semgrep SSRF rule exists but does not currently fire on this code shape, so this is tracked as manual_static; production ships a static SPA with no server, so the route is not reachable in production today.
findings.compensatingControl: Express sidecars are DEV-only (default OFF), never deployed; production is the static vite build only.
findings.removalCriterion: M3 eliminates/firewalls the DEV sidecar + egress host allowlist; SSRF regression test green.

findings.rationale: DEV Shipping API routes and identity/resolve write have no auth middleware. DEV-only, not deployed.
findings.compensatingControl: DEV sidecars default OFF and never deployed.
findings.removalCriterion: M3 shared enforced auth/CSRF/rate-limit middleware; no unauthenticated state-changing/secret route reachable in any deployed env.

findings.rationale: Carrier credentials held in an unauthenticated in-memory Map. DEV sidecar only, not deployed.
findings.compensatingControl: DEV-only; no persisted secrets; sidecar never deployed.
findings.removalCriterion: M3 encrypted secret store + rotation/KMS; no in-memory unauth credential store.

findings.rationale: Hardcoded refund PIN '1234' in the client authorization path. The Semgrep hardcoded-pin rule fires on POS.tsx but not this comparison shape, so tracked as manual_static.
findings.compensatingControl: Mock/DEV client-authoritative SPA; no real money movement wired.
findings.removalCriterion: M5 server-side refund/operator authorization; all hardcoded PIN instances removed.

findings.rationale: Hardcoded refund PIN '1234' in the POS refund path — detected by the tmpos-hardcoded-pin Semgrep rule.
findings.compensatingControl: Mock/DEV POS; no real settlement wired.
findings.removalCriterion: M7b server-side refund authorization; hardcoded PIN removed.

findings.rationale: Authorization derived client-side from a Firestore user doc role + sessionStorage. Known architectural gap; the whole point of Phase 4.
findings.compensatingControl: Fail-closed Firestore rules (proven by the M2 emulator suite 41/41) prevent client mutation of the role field.
findings.removalCriterion: M5 server-derived authorization is source of truth; no server decision depends on client-supplied role/entitlement/status.

findings.dependencyChain: FLAGGED instance: firebase@12.11.0 (client SDK) > @firebase/firestore@4.13.0 > @grpc/grpc-js@1.9.15. PATCHED server copy: firebase-admin@13.10.0 > @google-cloud/firestore > google-gax > @grpc/grpc-js@1.14.4 (>=1.9.16, not the flagged version).

reachabilityAnalysis.browserBundle: absent — @firebase/firestore uses WebChannel in the browser build; grpc-js is not bundled (build-integrity scan confirms no server modules in dist).
reachabilityAnalysis.serverIndexReachable: no — server/index.ts (Express Shipping sidecar) does not import firebase-admin or the client firebase SDK.
reachabilityAnalysis.platformIdentityReachable: yes, PATCHED copy only — server/platform-identity/ loads firebase-admin > google-gax > @grpc/grpc-js@1.14.4, which is NOT the flagged 1.9.15.
reachabilityAnalysis.vulnerableFunctionInvoked: no — the flagged 1.9.15 instance rides only under the client `firebase` SDK; no server code imports the client firebase SDK, and the browser build excludes grpc-js.
reachabilityAnalysis.exploitPreconditions: Advisory requires processing attacker-controlled inbound gRPC frames/metadata; the DEV sidecar performs only outbound calls to Google/Firebase endpoints.

findings.reachabilityEvidence: Classified server_runtime (a running DEV Node sidecar does load the grpc-js package family) rather than not_reachable, to avoid over-claiming — though the copy the server loads is the patched 1.14.4 and the flagged 1.9.15 is client-SDK-carried and browser-excluded.
findings.compensatingControl: Flagged instance not in the client bundle; server Firebase Admin is DEV-only and loads the patched copy.
findings.removalCriterion: Resolve @grpc/grpc-js to >=1.9.16 across the tree via transitive update/override; npm audit clears.

findings.dependencyChain: FLAGGED instance: @google/genai@1.46.0 > ws@8.19.0 (in advisory range). OTHER instance: firebase-tools@15.23.0 > ws@7.5.12 (dev CLI, 7.x — outside the advisory range).

reachabilityAnalysis.browserBundle: absent — browsers use the native WebSocket; `ws` is Node-only.
reachabilityAnalysis.serverIndexReachable: no — server/index.ts imports neither @google/genai nor ws.
reachabilityAnalysis.buildToolReachable: no — the firebase-tools > ws@7.5.12 copy is the dev CLI and is a different major outside the advisory range.
reachabilityAnalysis.vulnerableFunctionInvoked: no — the only in-range instance is @google/genai > ws@8.19.0, and @google/genai is imported by ZERO files (repo-wide grep of all .ts/.tsx/.js/.mjs excluding node_modules/dist — the pattern also matches dynamic import() strings — = 0 matches). @google/genai is a declared-but-unused production dependency.
reachabilityAnalysis.exploitPreconditions: n/a — the vulnerable package is not loaded by any code path.

findings.reachabilityEvidence: The flagged ws@8.19.0 rides only under @google/genai, which no source file imports; not present in the browser bundle. Classified not_reachable on proven absence of any import (not on bundle absence alone). Severity/blocker unchanged.
findings.rationale: Transitive Node WebSocket implementation under an unused dependency.
findings.compensatingControl: Not imported by any code path; not in the client bundle.
findings.removalCriterion: Remove the unused @google/genai dependency (or resolve ws to >=8.21.0); npm audit clears.

reachabilityAnalysis.serverIndexReachable: yes — server/index.ts imports express@4.22.1, which loads path-to-regexp@0.1.12 at route-registration time.
reachabilityAnalysis.vulnerableFunctionInvoked: yes (loaded/executed at router setup), but only over developer-defined static route patterns.
reachabilityAnalysis.exploitPreconditions: ReDoS requires attacker-influenced route PATTERNS (not request paths); the sidecar's routes are static and developer-defined. DEV sidecars are never deployed (production is the static SPA).

findings.reachabilityEvidence: Loaded by a running DEV Node server (Express) at route setup → server_runtime, not merely installed. Not downgraded despite the sidecar being DEV-only/undeployed; the missing exploit precondition (attacker-controlled route patterns) is documented.
findings.rationale: Transitive under the DEV Express sidecar.
findings.compensatingControl: DEV sidecars not deployed; routes are static/developer-defined.
findings.removalCriterion: Resolve path-to-regexp to >=0.1.13 via express update/override; npm audit clears.

findings.dependencyChain: vite@6.4.3 (fdir / tinyglobby) + vitest@4.1.10 > picomatch@4.0.3. OTHER instance: firebase-tools > chokidar > picomatch@2.3.2 (dev CLI, 2.x — outside the advisory range).

reachabilityAnalysis.buildToolReachable: yes — vite (fdir/tinyglobby) and vitest load picomatch@4.0.3 at build/test time.
reachabilityAnalysis.vulnerableFunctionInvoked: yes at build time, over developer-controlled globs only.
reachabilityAnalysis.exploitPreconditions: ReDoS requires attacker-controlled glob patterns; only build-time developer globs are processed. Not in shipped output.

findings.reachabilityEvidence: picomatch@4.0.3 is a build/glob-tooling dependency (vite/vitest); not imported by application runtime code and not in the client bundle.
findings.compensatingControl: Build-time only; not in shipped output.
findings.removalCriterion: Resolve picomatch to >=4.0.4 via transitive update/override; npm audit clears.

findings.rationale: A vendored third-party Claude-plugin marketplace bundle (1108 tracked files, own LICENSE/marketplace.json, not TM POS2026 app source) is committed to the repo. A repository-only Gitleaks scan surfaces EXACTLY 31 documentation-example credentials in its zoom-plugin skills guides (verified placeholders: literal CLIENT_ID:CLIENT_SECRET curl examples and sample JSON access_token values). Reconciled finding-by-finding (rule+path+line) against the expectedFindings inventory above — any 32nd finding, a new line, a new rule, or an app finding fails CI. Not live, not imported/bundled/executed by the app or CI.
findings.compensatingControl: Bundle is documentation/skills only; not referenced by package scripts, Vite, server code, runtime imports, or CI workflows. Exact-31 reconciliation prevents this entry from operating as a directory waiver.
findings.reconciliationBinding: Reconciliation binds each expected finding on (gitleaks rule, repository-relative path, line) AND binds every containing file (see containingFiles) to its whole-file Git blob OID. An in-place same-line secret substitution changes the file, so its OID no longer matches and the gate FAILS — closing the earlier content-blind gap without ever storing or hashing a secret value. A 32nd finding, a new line, a new rule, an app-path finding, a removed expected finding, or any containing-file OID change all fail.
findings.removalCriterion: Owner decision on whether the vendored bundle belongs in the repo; if retained, isolate or gitignore it. Not editable in M2 (out of authorized scope).

findings.rationale: The public-by-design Firebase web client identifier (a Google 'AIza…' apiKey shipped in the browser bundle — a client identifier, NOT a server secret). It surfaced when the broad AIza-keyspace allowlist was removed from .gitleaks.toml (that allowlist could have suppressed a genuinely NEW Google/Gemini/service key). Reconciled EXACTLY by rule id + repository-relative path + line and bound to firebase-applet-config.json's whole-file Git blob OID; two additional copies exist only in removed, historically-committed dist/ bundles (history-only). Any different/new AIza-style finding anywhere, an in-place same-line substitution (containing-file OID change), or removal of this finding fails the gate.
findings.compensatingControl: Firebase web apiKey is public client config (browser-shipped); it is not a credential. Access control is the fail-closed Firestore security rules (proven by the M2 emulator suite 41/41), not key secrecy. No server secret is exposed by this identifier.
findings.removalCriterion: None required for the identifier itself (public by design). This baseline is removed only if firebase-applet-config.json ceases to contain the client identifier; a real (non-public) secret introduced at this location would change the containing-file OID and fail the gate.
